biggest_tip: Practice graph traversals end-to-end. Don't skip Dijkstra under pressure.
round_1_context: Two coding problems on HackerRank. First was on Arrays/Hashing (find longest sub-array with k distinct elements). Second was a medium graph problem - shortest path with constraints. Both had partial test cases visible.
round_2_context: DSA round on Google Meet. They asked a hard DP problem: count distinct ways to partition an array such that sums of adjacent partitions differ by at most k. Solved with O(n*k) DP. Also asked about time complexity tradeoffs of memoization vs tabulation.
round_3_context: Mixed round - OOPs concepts (SOLID, when to use composition over inheritance with a real-world example) followed by a system design lite question: design a URL shortener.
round_4_context: Behavioral and team-fit. Walked through a project where I led a small team. Asked how I handled disagreement with a senior engineer.

biggest_tip: Communicate your thought process out loud. They care about reasoning more than solution correctness.
round_1_context: Live coding in Google Docs. One LeetCode medium-hard: given a 2D matrix, find the longest increasing path. I clarified edge cases, brute force first, then memoized DFS. Discussed time/space.
round_2_context: Two questions back-to-back. First on tries (implement autocomplete). Second on heaps (k closest points to origin). Both LeetCode-style.
round_3_context: Design a notification system at Google scale. Discussed pub-sub, fanout patterns, retry/backoff, rate limiting per user, monitoring. They probed deeply on consistency vs availability tradeoffs.
round_4_context: Googleyness round. Conflict resolution, what motivates me, why Google over other offers. Be specific - they push for concrete examples with measurable outcomes.
